rulings.Case Title: LAPASIA COLBERT VS. BIJOU, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025, line 7, DEFENDANT SFBSC MANAGEMENT, LLC DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT Defendant SFBSC Management, LLC's demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first through sixth, tenth and nineteenth causes of action and overruled as to the eight, eleventh, twenty-third and twenty-fourth causes of action. Plaintiffs' FEHA claims are time-barred because plaintiffs did not file an administrative complaint within three years of the conduct which they contend violates the FEHA and their 2024 administratie complaint does not relate back to their earlier filed administrative complaint. Plaintiff's PAGA claim is time-barred because their PAGA notice was submitted more than a year after the alleged PAGA violations and their 2024 PAGA does not relate back to their earlier submitted PAGA notice. Plaintiffs' conversion, wrongful termination, and battery claims are not time-barred because SFBC was brought into this case by Doe amendment on March 20, 2022 and these claims relate back to the filing of the initial complaint because they are based on the same general facts as alleged in the initial complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WEBCOR/OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE, VS. SKANSKA USA BUILDING INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFF WEBCOR/OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE, A JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT Plaintiff Webcor/Obayashi Joint Venture's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is denied. While California has a strong policy favoring the allowance of amendments to pleadings, one exception to that policy is where, as here, the proposed amendments lack substantive merit. Both of plaintiff's proposed new causes of action seek to reform (i.e. modify) a contract based on extrinsic evidence yet paragraph 12 of the contract sought to be reformed explicitly and unambiguously provides that "no parole or extrinsic evidence of any nature shall be used or be relevant to supplement, explain, or modify any term" of the contract. As explained in the order filed December 18, 2025 the quoted language is valid and enforceable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO CONSERVATORY OF MUSIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025, line 4, 1 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO CONSERVATORY OF MUSIC'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant San Francisco Conservatory of Music's demurrer to the fifth through eighth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to the fifth through eighth causes of action. Plaintiffs may file a second amended complaint no later than February 21, 2025. Although plaintiffs bring their claims per CCP 340.16(e) (FAC par. 63), they have not had adequately alleged that SFCM "engaged in a cover up or attempted a cover up of a prevous instance or allegations of sexual assault by an alleged perpetrator of such abuse" as required by that subsection. Moreover, the claims alleged by Jane Doe are all based on conduct that occurred before January 1, 2009 and thus, per CCP 340.16(b)(3), are not within the scope of the claims revived by CCP 340.16. Since it is reasonably possible that plaintiffs may be able to allege ultimate facts that remedy these deficiencies, they are given leave to do so, if they can do so in good faith. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: NORA HEIBER VS. AMERICAN GUILD OF MUSICAL ARTISTS, INC., A NEW ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANTS SAM WHEELER, ELLIOTT ENCARNACION, AMERICAN GUILD OF MUSICAL ARTISTS, INC., NED HANLON, SALLY MOUZON'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT (tentative ruling part 1 of 2) Defendants' alternative motion to compel arbitration of all causes of action in the first amended complaint is granted, the entirety of this case is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings, and defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint is taken off calendar as moot. Defendants' combined demurrer and motion to compel arbitration and alternative motion to compel arbitration is an unauthorized effort to seek a ruling on their various requests for relief in their desired order. The court declines defendants' request to rule on their demurrer to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint prior to ruling on their alternative request that the second cause of action be compelled to arbitration. Paragraph 15 of the parties' separation agreement requires the parties to arbitrate "all {covered] disputes" which includes the second cause of action, so it is violative of that paragraph for the court to rule on the demurrer to the second cause of action because, as stated below, the second cause of action is a dispute that is required to be arbitrated by the separation agreement. The separation agreement requires the parties to arbitrate "all disputes arising out of or relating to" the separation agreement. (Separation Agreement 15). The subject matter of the separation agreement is the termination of plaintiff Nora Heiber's employment with AGMA including what AGMA may say to others about the termination of that employment. (Separation Agreement 8(b), (e) and (f)). Both causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint arise out of and relate to the termination of Ms. Heiber's employment including the propriety of what AGMA has allegedly said about the termination of Ms. Heiber's employment and thus those causes of action are required to be arbitrated by the plain language of the separation agreement. Ms. Heiber's argument that her claims against the individual defendants are not arbitrable per the separation agreement lacks merit. Defendants correctly cite California case law that non-signatories to an arbitration agreement are covered by the agreement where, as here, the non-signatories are alleged to have acted as agents of a signatory of the agreement. (END OF TENATIVE RULING PART 1 SEE PART 2) = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CALLAHAN VS. ARTELA INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 24, 2025, line 9, DEFENDANT ARTELA INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Artela Inc.'s demurrer to all nine causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to all nine causes of action. Plaintiff Michael Callahan has leave to file a second amended complaint no later than February 21, 2025. Mr. Callahan's claims based on his own alleged disabilities are insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged that Artela knew that Mr. Callahan had disabilities. Mr. Callahan's claims based on retaliation are insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged that he engaged in any protected activities. Mr. Callahan's harassment claim is insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged a hostile environment. Mr. Callahan's derivative claims are insufficient because they depend on his other claims which are insufficient. Mr. Callahan is granted leave to amend because his opposition memorandum suggests that he may be able to remedy these insufficiencies in an amended complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
